title: Althaea tholozanii
intro: Althaea tholozanii, also known as Tholozan's marshmallow, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various ailments such as digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Althaea tholozanii include mucilages, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are often used to soothe digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems.

The classification of Althaea tholozanii is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaves, flowers, and growth habits. As a member of the Malvaceae family, Althaea tholozanii shares similarities with other plants in the same family.
